metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students apply the engineering design process to solve problems, exploring structures, materials science, and technical design. This course emphasizes iterative design, testing, and refinement in creating solutions to practical engineering challenges.
metadata.general_description: An applied course in engineering design, structures, materials, and technical problem-solving.

big_ideas.statement: The engineering design process provides a systematic approach to identifying problems, developing solutions, and improving designs through iteration.
big_ideas.elaboration: Structured design thinking and methodical testing and refinement lead to effective solutions that meet specified requirements.

big_ideas.statement: Materials have specific properties that determine their suitability for different applications and environmental conditions.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding material properties—strength, durability, cost, environmental impact—enables appropriate material selection for designs.

big_ideas.statement: Structural design must balance aesthetic, functional, and economic considerations while ensuring safety and stability.
big_ideas.elaboration: Analysis of forces, moments, and loads enables design of structures that are safe, efficient, and appropriate for their purpose.

big_ideas.statement: Successful engineering requires understanding constraints, making trade-offs, and considering environmental and social impacts.
big_ideas.elaboration: Responsible engineering considers sustainability, safety, cost-effectiveness, and broader impacts on communities and environments.

competencies.raw: Apply the engineering design process to solve problems
i_can_statements: I can define engineering problems and specifications
I can develop multiple design solutions
I can evaluate designs against criteria
I can iterate and refine designs through testing

competencies.raw: Design structures that are safe and efficient
i_can_statements: I can analyze loads and forces in structures
I can select structural forms and materials
I can calculate structural elements for safety
I can design for stability and appropriate factors of safety

competencies.raw: Apply material science knowledge to design and selection
i_can_statements: I can identify material properties and characteristics
I can select materials for specific applications
I can understand material processing and modification
I can consider sustainability and environmental factors

competencies.raw: Evaluate designs for feasibility, cost, and impact
i_can_statements: I can assess technical feasibility of designs
I can evaluate cost and resource requirements
I can analyze environmental and social impacts
I can recommend design improvements

competencies.raw: Build and test prototypes and models
i_can_statements: I can construct prototypes and working models
I can plan and conduct testing procedures
I can measure and collect test data
I can troubleshoot and refine prototypes
I can calculate forces and moments
I can perform stress and strain analysis
I can determine structural requirements
I can verify designs meet safety standards

competencies.raw: Select and work with materials appropriately
i_can_statements: I can test and evaluate material properties
I can work with various materials safely
I can apply material processing techniques
I can understand material limitations and failures
I can create technical drawings and specifications
I can write design reports and documentation
I can present designs and findings
I can communicate design rationale and decisions

competencies.raw: Use computer-aided design and analysis tools
i_can_statements: I can use CAD software for design
I can use simulation and finite element analysis
I can create and analyze designs virtually
I can generate technical drawings from models
I can use measurement tools and instruments
I can perform materials testing
I can use sensors and data collection equipment
I can analyze test data and results
I can operate machine tools safely
I can perform welding and metalwork
I can use hand tools and power tools
I can fabricate structural and mechanical components
I can assess new design and analysis tools
I can understand additive manufacturing (3D printing)
I can evaluate advanced materials and composites
I can identify engineering innovations

areas.name: Structural Analysis and Design
topics: Factor of safety and design standards
